notes: Mistral Vibe maintains a single-session task list via the builtin todo tool (action:'write' = full-list replace; TodoStatus pending|in_progress|completed|cancelled; auto-executes with no permission gate). The driver asks vibe to create three todos and walk each pending→in_progress→completed. The lifecycle arc (ready→working→ready) and SubagentCount=0 are observable and asserted as phases. KNOWN FAILING: the load-bearing tasks-content observable — three items in the session API tasks field with matching subjects + status — is NOT produced because core/adapters/inbound/agents/vibe/parser.go surfaces a todo call as a generic ToolUse{Name:'todo'} and never decodes its todos[] arguments into TaskDeltas/TaskSnapshot (the on-disk transcript carries the full list, so this is a parser bug, not an architectural incapability; OpenCode's todowrite is the reference fix). The spec asserts CORRECT behavior; record ports the driver's wait_turn seam, files the issue, and records known_failing. Observations assert tokens + model + agent only — Vibe carries no per-turn usage/cost, so cost is not asserted.

text: Vibe session (session_<ts>_<hex> dir) appears in ready; single-birth adapter so the first phase is anchored UNPINNED to start
PID bound shortly after launch via the CommandPattern matcher + DiscoverPID
User prompt drives the session ready → working

invariants: no state_transition to waiting for session
text: Session stays working across the todo create/write calls — todo is ToolPermission.ALWAYS (auto-executed, no permission gate fires); no child sessions spawn (SubagentCount stays 0)
Terminal no-tool assistant message drives turn_done: working → ready; final session state is ready; no child sessions were spawned
